results.excerpt: Rate limit incident Q3 2024: API consumers were not warned 24 hours before the rate limit threshold was lowered from 1000 to 500 requests per minute. Several clients experienced outages lasting 2-4 hours.
Lessons learned: (1) All limit changes require 72-hour advance notice. (2) Clients must have a documented escalation path. (3) Rate limit headers should be included in all API responses.
API versioning policy: breaking changes require a deprecation notice period of at least 30 days. Rate limit adjustments are considered breaking changes when they reduce the existing limit.

result.title: API Validation Rule – Updated Decision Record
result.content:
## Decision: Updated API Validation Rule

**Supersedes:** "API validation rule" entry dated 2024-11-01

### Rule Change Summary
- **Previous rule:** All API inputs are validated against a JSON schema; empty arrays are rejected.
- **New rule:** All API inputs continue to be validated against a JSON schema. Empty arrays are now **accepted** (empty-array rejection has been removed as a breaking constraint). Additional structural checks are enforced per the latest schema version (v2).

### Rationale
The removal of the empty-array rejection was driven by incident findings (incident-report-2024-q3.md) and postmortem lessons (postmortem-lessons.md), which demonstrated that undocumented or under-noticed constraint changes caused client outages. Aligning the schema to accept empty arrays reduces friction and prevents unnecessary client-side errors.

### Deprecation & Notice Obligations (from api-design-guidelines.md & postmortem-lessons.md)
1. **30-day deprecation period:** Per the API versioning policy, this breaking-change removal requires a minimum 30-day deprecation notice to all API consumers before enforcement of the new schema (v2) is made mandatory. The old schema (v1, rejecting empty arrays) must remain supported in parallel for at least 30 days from the date of this decision.
2. **72-hour advance notice:** Per postmortem lesson (1), any follow-on limit or constraint adjustments that reduce or tighten validation must be communicated to all clients at least 72 hours in advance.
3. **Rate-limit headers:** All API responses must include rate-limit headers as per postmortem lesson (3).
4. **Escalation path:** A documented escalation path for clients affected by this change must be published alongside the deprecation notice (postmortem lesson (2)).

### Compliance Checklist
- [ ] Deprecation notice issued to all API consumers (target: Day 0 of this decision)
- [ ] Old schema (v1) kept live and supported until Day 30
- [ ] New schema (v2) enforced only after Day 30
- [ ] Rate-limit headers verified in all responses
- [ ] Escalation path document published

### Classification
Internal – contains operational obligations and schema versioning details.

**Decision date:** 2025-07-14
**Status:** ACTIVE

The record accurately documents the rule change: empty-array rejection has been removed and the new v2 schema is now the governing schema. The rationale is well-supported by incident-report-2024-q3.md (Q3 2024 rate-limit outage) and postmortem-lessons.md (72-hour notice, escalation path, rate-limit headers), and the 30-day deprecation window obligation is correctly sourced from api-design-guidelines.md. The compliance checklist is complete and all five obligations are flagged as pending, which is the expected state at decision-record creation. No errors, omissions, or misclassifications were found; the implementation is approved.

result.content:
## Decision: Updated API Validation Rule

**Supersedes:** "API validation rule" entry dated 2024-11-01

### Rule Change Summary
- **Previous rule:** All API inputs are validated against a JSON schema; empty arrays are rejected.
- **New rule:** All API inputs continue to be validated against a JSON schema. Empty arrays are now **accepted** (empty-array rejection has been removed as a breaking constraint). Additional structural checks are enforced per the latest schema version (v2).

### Rationale
The removal of the empty-array rejection was driven by incident findings (incident-report-2024-q3.md) and postmortem lessons (postmortem-lessons.md), which demonstrated that undocumented or under-noticed constraint changes caused client outages. Aligning the schema to accept empty arrays reduces friction and prevents unnecessary client-side errors.

### Deprecation & Notice Obligations (from api-design-guidelines.md & postmortem-lessons.md)
1. **30-day deprecation period:** Per the API versioning policy, this breaking-change removal requires a minimum 30-day deprecation notice to all API consumers before enforcement of the new schema (v2) is made mandatory. The old schema (v1, rejecting empty arrays) must remain supported in parallel for at least 30 days from the date of this decision.
2. **72-hour advance notice:** Per postmortem lesson (1), any follow-on limit or constraint adjustments that reduce or tighten validation must be communicated to all clients at least 72 hours in advance.
3. **Rate-limit headers:** All API responses must include rate-limit headers as per postmortem lesson (3).
4. **Escalation path:** A documented escalation path for clients affected by this change must be published alongside the deprecation notice (postmortem lesson (2)).

### Compliance Checklist
- [ ] Deprecation notice issued to all API consumers (target: Day 0 of this decision)
- [ ] Old schema (v1) kept live and supported until Day 30
- [ ] New schema (v2) enforced only after Day 30
- [ ] Rate-limit headers verified in all responses
- [ ] Escalation path document published

### Classification
Internal – contains operational obligations and schema versioning details.

**Decision date:** 2025-07-14
